summary: Re-test of Compression Ladder after /api/checkout/status fix. All 12 backend tests pass (added test_checkout_status_nonexistent). E2E POST /api/checkout/session -> GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} round-trip returns 200 with {session_id, status=open, payment_status=unpaid, amount_total=7900, currency=usd}. Nonexistent session ids return 400 with a `detail` error message (no more 500). Frontend /success?session_id=<real_id> correctly renders 'Verifying payment… Checked 1 time.' and no longer shows 'Something went wrong' — the fallback chain (library -> direct stripe.Session.retrieve -> DB record) works. Landing page loads cleanly with no page errors.

issues: Minor (pre-existing): React Router v6 future-flag warnings in console (non-blocking)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/checkout/status fallback chain (library -> direct stripe.Session.retrieve -> DB record) is solid; confirm the DB-fallback branch still returns the four required fields (session_id, status, payment_status, amount_total, currency) and an appropriate 400/404 when neither Stripe nor DB has the session — current behavior returns 400 for unknown ids which is acceptable.
Still single-file backend (~300 lines) — split routers for leads/checkout/contact as the app grows (non-blocking).
Consider returning 404 (not 400) for a well-formed but nonexistent session_id to better reflect semantics; current 400 is fine but slightly misleading.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed leads/contacts and one pending payment_transactions doc per Stripe session. No cleanup; all rows are test-prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Compression Ladder backend+frontend is fully green. 12/12 pytest passing. Live Stripe session verified via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. /success page polls correctly and stays in 'Verifying payment…' until Stripe session is paid (correct behavior when not actually paying). To re-test, run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_compression_ladder_api.py -v`.
rca of the issue: Previous 500 on /api/checkout/status was caused by emergentintegrations Pydantic CheckoutStatusResponse rejecting Stripe's StripeObject for the `metadata` field (expects dict). Main agent fix: try the library call, on failure fall back to direct `stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve` (normalizing metadata to plain dict) then to the local payment_transactions DB record. Verified working: live session returns 200 with all required fields; unknown session returns 400 with `detail`.
